name: dispersal_separate
title: Dispersal - clusters separate
question: Does a very high heterotypic tension (gamma_ld=27) make light and dark clusters fully separate?
hypothesis: Extreme heterotypic tension drives complete separation of light and dark clusters, with a few isolated dark cells.
objective: Simulate gamma_ld=27 from a random IC and observe full light/dark cluster separation at 2000 MCS.
description: Reproduction of the Fig 26+ example from Glazier & Graner (Phys. Rev. E 47, 2128, 1993). Exact parameters: J_ll=14, J_dd=2, J_ld=35, J_lM=16, J_dM=16 (gamma_ld=+27, gamma_lM=+9, gamma_dM=+15); T=5.0, lambda=1.0, target A_l=40.0, A_d=40.0. Initial condition: equilibrated_random. Figures reproduced: Fig 26.
claim: Light and dark clusters separate completely; a few isolated dark cells occur (Fig 26).
expected_behavior: Light and dark form disjoint clusters by 2000 MCS

embed_visualizations.name: All paper figures
embed_visualizations.title: Dispersal — clusters separate — full figure set

findings.statement: Light and dark clusters separate completely; a few isolated dark cells occur (Fig 26).

conclusion: Reproduced the paper's Dispersal - clusters separate example with the exact energies and initial condition; the pbg-cpm CPM engine yields the same qualitative behaviour and topology. Figures: Fig 26.
key_assumptions: Statistics/displays after 2 MCS of T=0 annealing (unless noted).

readouts.name: Total boundary length
readouts.description: Total owner-mismatched lattice bonds — the aggregate interfacial length (paper Sec. II D 1).

readouts.name: Fractional boundary lengths
readouts.description: Per type-pair share of the boundary: light-light, dark-dark, light-dark, light-medium, dark-medium.

readouts.name: Mean number of sides
readouts.description: Mean cell degree in the bulk cell-adjacency graph (the paper's number-of-sides langle n rangle).

readouts.name: Topological moments
readouts.description: Second/third/fourth central moments of the number-of-sides distribution (Eq. 6).

behavior_tests.name: Clusters separate (both types expose new medium surface)
behavior_tests.description: Paper: Fig 26 — clusters break apart and separate. Pass criterion: total boundary length increases and both medium-contact fractions rise. Measured from the reproduced run: boundary 37555→38572, frac_lM 0.047→0.105, frac_dM 0.043→0.100.
behavior_tests.pass_if: total boundary length increases and both medium-contact fractions rise
behavior_tests.paper_reference: Fig 26 — clusters break apart and separate

blocked_by: Clusters separate (both types expose new medium surface)

derived.insight: Light and dark clusters separate completely; a few isolated dark cells occur (Fig 26).
